summary: Completed frontend-only regression for recurring invoice feature set and related invoice flows. All requested user flows passed end-to-end; one console-level markup warning was observed.

issues: React console warning on invalid DOM nesting: <span> cannot be a child of <option> (hydration-risk warning).

action_items: Investigate and remove invalid nesting in <select><option> rendering on recurring source invoice options to eliminate hydration warning.
Optional: add CI guard to fail on React invalid DOM nesting console errors during E2E runs.
critical_code_review_comments: App.css remains very large (>1200 lines observed) and should be split by feature/screen for maintainability.
Recurring option rendering emits runtime DOM nesting warning; even if non-blocking now, this can cause hydration issues in stricter render paths.
seed_data_creation: No file-based seed data. LocalStorage demo data was reset and regenerated during UI tests.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Previously reported invoice row View navigation issue is no longer reproducible; recurring create/edit/pause-resume/generate/delete and route transitions all passed. Validate after any rendering-layer change that recurring source select no longer logs invalid <option> child warning.
rca of the issue: Reproduction: open app fresh and navigate to recurring page; browser console logs React warning 'In HTML, <span> cannot be a child of <option>' with stack pointing to recurring source select option rendering. Likely root cause is transformed JSX output injecting span wrappers into option children during build/runtime instrumentation. Mitigation: render plain string content only inside <option> and ensure no wrappers/components are injected.
